CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, department VARCHAR);
SELECT name, department, dense_rank() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY id) AS department_rank FROM employees;
CREATE TABLE sales (transaction_id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, amount DECIMAL);
SELECT e.name, dense_rank() OVER (ORDER BY s.amount DESC) AS sales_rank FROM employees e JOIN sales s ON e.id = s.employee_id;
CREATE TABLE projects (project_id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, project_name VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1, 1, 'Project A'), (2, 2, 'Project B'), (3, 3, 'Project C'), (4, 4, 'Project D');
SELECT p.project_name, e.name, dense_rank() OVER (PARTITION BY p.project_name ORDER BY e.id) AS project_rank FROM projects p JOIN employees e ON p.employee_id = e.id;
SELECT department, COUNT(*) AS count, dense_rank() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC) AS dept_rank FROM employees GROUP BY department;
CREATE TABLE performance (employee_id INTEGER, score INTEGER);
SELECT e.name, p.score, dense_rank() OVER (ORDER BY p.score DESC) AS score_rank FROM employees e JOIN performance p ON e.id = p.employee_id;
SELECT e.name, dense_rank() OVER (PARTITION BY e.department ORDER BY p.score DESC) AS department_score_rank FROM employees e JOIN performance p ON e.id = p.employee_id;
